Case 45+2: what does the evidence say?

Visual replay

The sudden drop of the ball and Norway’s immediate reaction support the contact theory.

Ball sensor

FIFA said the chip showed no spike, so contact was not confirmed.

IFAB law

If the ball touches an outside agent, play is stopped and restarted with a dropped ball at the point of contact.

Model verdictGOAL SHOULD BE DISALLOWED
Debate pulse
🇳🇴 Norway

“We were robbed”, “rules are rules”, distrust of VAR — yet pride in a historic tournament.

🏴 England

“We got lucky”, “play to the whistle”, admiration for Bellingham’s run — and debate whether one episode should decide the match.

🌍 Global internet

The main demand: slow motion, more angles, TV picture vs sensor comparison — and a way to test the trajectory yourself.

Player positions are an approximate reconstruction from the wide replay; the physical kick parameters are preserved unchanged.
